what_it_does: This file is a draft noticeboard document that frames an Obsidian-as-host exploration and lists an ordered sequence of related board item addresses for block-addressable commentary.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board:// address, type=document, source=claude_code, state=draft, channel=dragnet-development, and an empty thread and links array.
An 'order' array contains eleven board:// item references.
Body text reads: 'An Obsidian-as-host exploration (one lens). Block-addressable — comment on any part.'
History shows two transitions: filed from null to draft, then an edit to the order field.
Created and updated timestamps are identical to sub-second precision.
standouts: Frames the content as 'one lens', implying multiple possible perspectives.
Provides an explicit ordered list of related board items.
Invites block-addressable comments on any part.
Draft state with minimal body content.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML metadata and a Markdown body.
Uses board:// URI identifiers for items.
Includes an audit history with actor session and timestamp.
Uses an 'order' field to express a curated sequence.
concerns: Body is very thin and mostly restates the title.
The purpose or narrative connecting the eleven ordered items is not explained.
The 'links' array is empty even though 'order' contains related references.
'One lens' hints at sibling lenses but none are referenced here.
notes: A draft index-style noticeboard item that introduces an Obsidian-host exploration perspective and points to a curated sequence of related board items.
questions: What exactly does 'Obsidian-as-host' mean in this project context?
What criterion determined the ordering of the eleven board items?
Why is the 'links' field empty when 'order' contains related references?
What is the scope of the dragnet-development channel?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an Obsidian-host exploration, but the file behaves more like a labeled lens or reading index with a sparse body and an ordered list of references.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to establish a named viewpoint and a navigable, commentable sequence for examining an Obsidian-as-host design space.
novelty: Explicit 'one lens' framing that acknowledges multiple viewpoints, plus an open invitation to comment on any block.
contribution: Provides a labeled perspective and an ordered set of related board references that a fused system could retain as a navigational entry point for the Obsidian-host exploration.
summary_for_embedding: Draft noticeboard document titled 'Existing footprint — Obsidian-host exploration' that frames an Obsidian-as-host lens and lists eleven related board item addresses for block-addressable commentary.
intention_for_embedding: To create a curated, commentable index for exploring how Obsidian could serve as a host environment within the project.
